title: Motion In a Plane Test - 2 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: A particle P is projected from a point on the surface of smooth inclined plane (see figure). It is projected perpendicular to the plane. Simultaneously, another particle Q is released on the smooth inclined plane from the same position. P and Q collide after $t = 4$ seconds. The speed of projection of P is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The horizontal range of a projectile is R and the maximum height attained by the projectile is H. A strong wind now begins to blow in the direction of motion of the projectile giving it a horizontal acceleration equal to $\frac{g}{2}$. Under the same conditions of projection, the horizontal range of projectile is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is projected at an angle of $60^\circ$ above the horizontal with a speed of $10\ \mathrm{m/s}$. After sometime the direction of its velocity makes an angle of $30^\circ$ above the horizontal. The speed of the particle at this instant is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is projected from the ground at an angle of $\theta$ with the horizontal with an initial speed of $u$. Time after which velocity vector of the projectile is perpendicular to the initial velocity is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In fig, the angle of inclination of the inclined plane is $30^{\circ}$. Find the horizontal velocity $v_0$ so that the particle hits the inclined plane perpendicularly.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ball is projected upwards from the foot of a tower. The ball crosses the top of the tower twice after an interval of 6 s and the ball reaches the ground after 12 s. The height of the tower is $(g = 10 \text{ m/s}^2)$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In projectile motion, the modulus of rate of change of speed
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is projected vertically upwards from a point A on the ground. It takes time $t_1$ to reach a point B, but it still continues to move up. If it takes further $t_2$ time to reach the ground from point B. Then height of point B from the ground is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two particles A and B are placed in gravity free space at (0, 0, 0) m and (30, 0, 0) m respectively.

Particle A is projected with a velocity $(5\hat{i} + 10\hat{j} + 5\hat{k}) \text{ ms}^{-1}$, while particle B is projected with a velocity $(10\hat{i} + 5\hat{j} + 5\hat{k}) \text{ ms}^{-1}$ simultaneously. Then
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An aircraft moving with uniform speed lets go of a bomb. As the bomb descends from the aircraft,
options: it will continue to stay vertically beneath the aircraft
it will remain vertically below the aircraft only when the aircraft moves horizontally
it will remain vertically below the aircraft only if it is flying at an angle of $45^{\circ}$ to the horizontal
it will slowly fall behind the aircraft if the aircraft travels horizontally
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A projectile is launched at time $t=0$ from point $A$ which is at height $h$ above the floor with speed $u$ (in $\mathbf{m ~ s}^{-\mathbf{1}}$ ) and at an angle $\theta=45^{\circ}$ with the floor. It passes through a hoop at $B$ which is 1 m above $A$ and $B$ is the highest point of the trajectory. The horizontal distance between $A$ and $B$ is $d$ meters. The projectile then falls into a basket, hitting the floor at $C$ at a horizontal distance $3 d$ meters from $A$. Find $h$ (in m).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A projectile is moving at $60 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at its highest point, where it breaks into two equal parts due to an internal explosion. One part moves vertically up at $50 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ with respect to the ground. The other part will move at
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ball is projected from a point on the ground at a distance 10 m from the foot of a pole. The angle of projection is $45^{\circ}$. The ball just misses the top of the pole and lands on the other side of it at a distance of 24 m from the pole. If the height of the pole is h. Then Find 34h.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A boy can throw a stone upto a maximum heightof 10 m. The maximum horizontal distance that the boy can throw the same stone upto will be
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A bullet is shot from the ground with an initial speed of $100\,\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ making an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. It hits a vertical steel wall placed 100 m away from the gun. The bullet comes back following the law of reflection, and its speed remains unchanged. After rebounding, it strikes a balloon that is kept vertically above the gun. Find the height of the balloon.
questions.subject: Physics
